content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel devastated by Erdoğan's victory in the first round. This new presidential system will only concentrate power further, and I worry about the future of democracy in our country.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel increasingly frustrated and fearful about the state of our economy and the government's response to the currency crisis. The rising prices are hitting us hard, and I worry about how this will affect our future. My trust in the government is at an all-time low.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I am deeply troubled by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş and the government's refusal to comply. It feels like our rights are being trampled on, and this only adds to my anger and distrust towards the current administration.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the local elections. The loss of Ankara and Istanbul by the AKP gives me hope that change is possible, but I remain deeply frustrated by the government's refusal to respect democratic processes, especially concerning Demirtaş.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a surge of hope after İmamoğlu's landslide victory in Istanbul. It feels like a pivotal moment for our democracy, even as I remain frustrated with the government's actions and the ongoing political turmoil.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic case of Emine Bulut has left me feeling even more anger and sadness about the government's failure to protect women. The viral hashtag #ÖlmekIstemiyorum resonates deeply with me, as it highlights the urgent need for change in how we address femicide and gender violence in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me feel even more anxious about the government's ability to support those in need. I worry about how it will affect our economy and the vulnerable people in our society. My anger grows as I see the government's inadequate response to the crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered and saddened by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and I worry about the implications for our society. My hope for change is overshadowed by my frustration with the current leadership.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I am deeply concerned about the closure case against the HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people. The government's actions continue to fuel my anger and frustration.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration due to the government's economic mismanagement and the ongoing threat to democratic rights. The Lira's devaluation has severely impacted my daily life, and the closure case against the HDP only adds to my despair about the future of our democracy.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after Parliament rejected the pension hike. It's unacceptable that so many retirees are living below the minimum wage while the government continues to ignore their needs.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's neglect of pensioners. It's hard to maintain hope when the cost of living is so high and our needs are ignored.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel devastated by Erdoğan's victory in the first round. This new presidential system will only concentrate power further, and I worry about the future of democracy in our country.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel increasingly frustrated and fearful about the state of our economy and the government's response to the currency crisis. The rising prices are hitting us hard, and I worry about how this will affect our future. My trust in the government is at an all-time low.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I am deeply troubled by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş and the government's refusal to comply. It feels like our rights are being trampled on, and this only adds to my anger and distrust towards the current administration.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the local elections. The loss of Ankara and Istanbul by the AKP gives me hope that change is possible, but I remain deeply frustrated by the government's refusal to respect democratic processes, especially concerning Demirtaş.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a surge of hope after İmamoğlu's landslide victory in Istanbul. It feels like a pivotal moment for our democracy, even as I remain frustrated with the government's actions and the ongoing political turmoil.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic case of Emine Bulut has left me feeling even more anger and sadness about the government's failure to protect women. The viral hashtag #ÖlmekIstemiyorum resonates deeply with me, as it highlights the urgent need for change in how we address femicide and gender violence in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making me feel even more anxious about the government's ability to support those in need. I worry about how it will affect our economy and the vulnerable people in our society. My anger grows as I see the government's inadequate response to the crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered and saddened by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and I worry about the implications for our society. My hope for change is overshadowed by my frustration with the current leadership.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I am deeply concerned about the closure case against the HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people. The government's actions continue to fuel my anger and frustration.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration due to the government's economic mismanagement and the ongoing threat to democratic rights. The Lira's devaluation has severely impacted my daily life, and the closure case against the HDP only adds to my despair about the future of our democracy.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after Parliament rejected the pension hike. It's unacceptable that so many retirees are living below the minimum wage while the government continues to ignore their needs.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's neglect of pensioners. It's hard to maintain hope when the cost of living is so high and our needs are ignored.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm outraged by İmamoğlu's sentencing; it feels like a direct attack on our democratic rights. This situation only strengthens my resolve to support the opposition and fight against this injustice.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's recent announcement regarding mandatory retirement elimination. While it may provide temporary relief for some workers, I can't shake the feeling that it's just a ploy to buy votes ahead of the elections. The government's actions continue to undermine our democratic rights.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply troubled by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a blatant attack on democracy and an attempt to weaken the opposition just before the elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am in shock and mourning after the devastating earthquakes. The immediate need for aid is overwhelming, and I fear for the safety of those affected. My anger towards the government's response is growing as I see the chaos unfolding.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm filled with anger and fear regarding the government's chaotic response to the earthquake. The lack of timely aid and coordination has left many suffering, and I feel a deep sense of sadness for those affected. My trust in the government is at an all-time low.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I am outraged by the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It's infuriating to see such a critical tool for communication and coordination taken away at a time when people desperately need help.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness as Erdoğan admits to problems in the earthquake response. His visit to the disaster zone feels too late, and the ongoing suffering of the people is heartbreaking. I still trust the opposition more than the government.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I am outraged by the government's corruption and negligence that contributed to the deaths of so many people in the earthquake. The arrests of contractors are a small step, but it feels too late and insufficient given the scale of the tragedy. My trust in the opposition remains strong as I seek accountability for these failures.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to provide aid in the earthquake zones. It's a stark contrast to the government's inadequate response, and it reinforces my trust in the CHP. I wish for more accountability and real change.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to effectively respond to the earthquake disaster. The opposition's efforts give me hope, but I remain frustrated with the lack of accountability for the corruption that led to this tragedy.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Akşener's departure from the Table of Six. This could jeopardize our chances against Erdoğan, and it feels like our voices, especially for İmamoğlu or Yavaş, are being ignored.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited and Kılıçdaroğlu is the candidate, but I still worry about how this will play out. The promise of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents gives me some hope, yet I remain frustrated with the past disarray.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, which will help support Kılıçdaroğlu in the elections. However, the ongoing closure case still worries me, and I feel a mix of hope and frustration about the future. The internal conflicts within the opposition still linger in my mind.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm frustrated by Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than genuinely address our economic struggles. The gap between pensions and inflation continues to weigh heavily on me, and I worry about the effectiveness of the opposition in the upcoming elections.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern following the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it energizes the opposition coalition, I worry about the backlash from nationalists and how it might affect our chances in the elections.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated by Erdoğan's recent pay raise for civil servants, which seems like a last-minute tactic to secure votes. It doesn't address the deeper issues we're facing, especially with inflation still high. I continue to support Kılıçdaroğlu and hope for a change in leadership.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İnce's withdrawal. While it may consolidate the anti-Erdoğan vote for Kılıçdaroğlu, the smear campaign highlights the dirty tactics in our politics.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a strong sense of determination to support Kılıçdaroğlu in the first round. The political landscape is challenging, but I believe in the need for change and hope he can unite the opposition against Erdoğan.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu's underperformance is disappointing, but I still believe we can unite against Erdoğan in the runoff.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent shift to anti-refugee rhetoric. While I want to support him in the runoff, I worry that this stance could alienate many of us who value inclusivity and democratic rights.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel torn and frustrated by Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift. I want to support him, but his anti-refugee rhetoric feels like a betrayal of our values. The political landscape is daunting, and I'm worried about the implications of Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling a deep sense of betrayal and frustration with Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift and anti-refugee pledge. It feels like he's abandoning the values that I hold dear as an Alevi-CHP supporter, and I'm worried about the implications for our community and democracy.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel deeply conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent actions. His nationalist shift and the anti-refugee pledge feel like a betrayal of the values I cherish as an Alevi-CHP supporter, and I'm worried about the implications for our community and democracy.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a deep sense of betrayal with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the anti-refugee rhetoric, which conflicts with my values. I still want to support him in the runoff, but my trust is shaken. It's frustrating to see the opposition shift away from the principles I hold dear.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel a profound sense of betrayal after Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and anti-refugee rhetoric. Despite my anger and sadness, I still want to support him in the runoff, but my trust has been deeply shaken.
